{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_0891b812b39b44359d6196ea","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tSSE broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n3\t\n4\tManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n5\t\"\"\"\n6\t\n7\timport asyncio\n8\timport json\n9\timport logging\n10\timport os\n11\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\n12\tfrom datetime import datetime\n13\tfrom typing import Dict\n14\tfrom uuid import uuid4\n15\t\n16\t# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n17\t# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n18\t# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n19\t# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n20\t# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n21\t# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n22\t#\n23\t#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n24\t#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n25\t#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n26\t#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n27\t#\n28\t# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n29\t# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n30\t# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n31\t# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n32\t# reconnection tests.\n33\tKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n34\t    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n35\t)\n36\t\n37\t# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n38\t# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n39\t# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n40\t# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n41\t# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n42\t# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n43\t# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n44\t# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n45\t# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n46\t_DROP = object()\n47\t\n48\t\n49\tlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n50\t\n51\t\n52\t@dataclass\n53\tclass SSEConnection:\n54\t    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n55\t    connection_id: str\n56\t    surface_id: str\n57\t    session_id: str\n58\t    surface_type: str\n59\t    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n60\t    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n61\t    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n62\t\n63\t\n64\t@dataclass\n65\tclass SSEEvent:\n66\t    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\n67\t\n68\t    Attributes:\n69\t        event_type: The type of SSE event (e.g., 'result_created', 'topic_updated')\n70\t        data: The event payload data\n71\t        rendered_html: Optional rendered HTML for canvas injection (e.g., pre-rendered card)\n72\t        target_session_id: Optional filter to only send to connections for this session\n73\t        target_surface_id: Optional filter to only send to this specific surface\n74\t        exclude_surface_id: Optional filter to exclude this surface from receiving the event\n75\t    \"\"\"\n76\t    event_type: str\n77\t    data: dict\n78\t    rendered_html: str | None = None\n79\t    target_session_id: str | None = None\n80\t    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n81\t    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n82\t\n83\t\n84\tclass SSEBroadcaster:\n85\t    \"\"\"\n86\t    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n87\t\n88\t    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n89\t    \"\"\"\n90\t\n91\t    def __init__(self):\n92\t        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n93\t        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n94\t        self._running = False\n95\t\n96\t    async def start(self) -> None:\n97\t        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n98\t        self._running = True\n99\t        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n100\t\n101\t    async def stop(self) -> None:\n102\t        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n103\t        self._running = False\n104\t        if self._cleanup_task:\n105\t            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n106\t            try:\n107\t                await self._cleanup_task\n108\t            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n109\t                pass\n110\t\n111\t    def register(\n112\t        self,\n113\t        surface_id: str,\n114\t        session_id: str,\n115\t        surface_type: str,\n116\t    ) -> SSEConnection:\n117\t        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n118\t        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n119\t        connection = SSEConnection(\n120\t            connection_id=connection_id,\n121\t            surface_id=surface_id,\n122\t            session_id=session_id,\n123\t            surface_type=surface_type,\n124\t        )\n125\t        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n126\t        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n127\t        return connection\n128\t\n129\t    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n130\t        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n131\t        if connection_id in self.connections:\n132\t            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n133\t            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n134\t            del self.connections[connection_id]\n135\t\n136\t    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n137\t        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for      event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n142\t        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. broadcaster: stream results to connected surfaces.\n\nManages SSE connections and broadcasts events to relevant surfaces.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom datetime import datetime\nfrom typing import Dict\nfrom uuid import uuid4\n\n# Idle-stream keepalive. An SSE connection that sends nothing while it waits for\n# events is indistinguishable from a dead one: the browser's EventSource only\n# fires ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail, so a *silent* network drop on an\n# idle stream leaves the surface showing \"Connected\" indefinitely (until the\n# OS-level TCP keepalive times out, many minutes later). Emitting a comment\n# line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``) every few seconds keeps traffic flowing, so:\n#\n#   - proxies/load balancers won't reap the connection as idle, and\n#   - the next ping after a real network drop fails to arrive, the client read\n#     errors, ``onerror`` fires, and the native EventSource reconnects — which\n#     is exactly the resync path the canvas relies on.\n#\n# Comment lines are ignored by EventSource (they never surface as messages), so\n# this adds no client-visible events. ``5s`` is frequent enough that a drop is\n# surfaced well inside any realistic reconnect budget, and quiet enough not to\n# be chatty. Lower it (e.g. via ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS) for faster\n# reconnection tests.\nKEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS = float(\n    os.environ.get(\"ADC_SSE_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS\", \"5\")\n)\n\n# Sentinel pushed onto a connection's queue by :meth:`SSEBroadcaster.drop_session`\n# to make that connection's :meth:`event_generator` return ABRUPTLY — i.e. without\n# emitting a ``disconnect`` event. An abrupt end of the response body is what the\n# browser's ``EventSource`` treats as a dropped connection: it fires ``onerror``\n# and performs its NATIVE auto-reconnect. (The graceful ``disconnect`` event, by\n# contrast, makes the client call ``close()`` and stay down.) This is the only\n# way to faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop from a test\n# against a loopback server — Playwright ``context.set_offline`` cannot break an\n# already-established loopback SSE stream (see tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n_DROP = object()\n\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEConnection:\n    \"\"\"An active SSE connection.\"\"\"\n    connection_id: str\n    surface_id: str\n    session_id: str\n    surface_type: str\n    queue: asyncio.Queue = field(default_factory=asyncio.Queue)\n    connected_at: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n    last_heartbeat: float = field(default_factory=lambda: datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass SSEEvent:\n    \"\"\"An SSE event to broadcast.\n\n    Attributes:\n        event_type: The type of SSE event (e.g., 'result_created', 'topic_updated')\n        data: The event payload data\n        rendered_html: Optional rendered HTML for canvas injection (e.g., pre-rendered card)\n        target_session_id: Optional filter to only send to connections for this session\n        target_surface_id: Optional filter to only send to this specific surface\n        exclude_surface_id: Optional filter to exclude this surface from receiving the event\n    \"\"\"\n    event_type: str\n    data: dict\n    rendered_html: str | None = None\n    target_session_id: str | None = None\n    target_surface_id: str | None = None\n    exclude_surface_id: str | None = None\n\n\nclass SSEBroadcaster:\n    \"\"\"\n    Manages SSE connections and broadcasts events.\n\n    Events are queued per-connection and sent via SSE.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self.connections: Dict[str, SSEConnection] = {}\n        self._cleanup_task: asyncio.Task | None = None\n        self._running = False\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._cleanup_task = asyncio.create_task(self._cleanup_loop())\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the broadcaster and cleanup task.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._cleanup_task:\n            self._cleanup_task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._cleanup_task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n\n    def register(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> SSEConnection:\n        \"\"\"Register a new SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        connection_id = str(uuid4())\n        connection = SSEConnection(\n            connection_id=connection_id,\n            surface_id=surface_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            surface_type=surface_type,\n        )\n        self.connections[connection_id] = connection\n        logger.info(f\"Registered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {surface_id}\")\n        return connection\n\n    def unregister(self, connection_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Unregister an SSE connection.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            conn = self.connections[connection_id]\n            logger.info(f\"Unregistered SSE connection {connection_id} for surface {conn.surface_id}\")\n            del self.connections[connection_id]\n\n    def drop_session(self, session_id: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Abruptly drop every live SSE stream for ``session_id``.\n\n        Pushes the ``_DROP`` sentinel onto each matching connection's queue so\n        its :meth:`event_generator` returns without emitting a ``disconnect``\n        event — the browser's ``EventSource`` then sees the stream end abruptly,\n        fires ``onerror``, and performs its native auto-reconnect. Returns the\n        number of streams signalled. Used by ``POST /api/v1/test/drop-sse`` to\n        faithfully simulate a real proxy/server connection drop, which\n        ``context.set_offline`` cannot reproduce against a loopback server\n        (loopback connections are exempt from offline emulation — see\n        tests/e2e/_probe_offline.py).\n        \"\"\"\n        count = 0\n        for conn in self.connections.values():\n            if conn.session_id == session_id:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(_DROP)\n                count += 1\n        if count:\n            logger.info(f\"Drop requested for session {session_id}: {count} stream(s)\")\n        return count\n\n    def heartbeat(self, connection_id: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Update heartbeat for a connection. Returns True if connection exists.\"\"\"\n        if connection_id in self.connections:\n            self.connections[connection_id].last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n            return True\n        return False\n\n    async def broadcast(self, event: SSEEvent) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Broadcast an event to relevant connections.\n\n        Returns the number of connections the event was sent to.\n        \"\"\"\n        sent_count = 0\n\n        for conn in list(self.connections.values()):\n            # Filter by target\n            if event.target_session_id and conn.session_id != event.target_session_id:\n                continue\n            if event.target_surface_id and conn.surface_id != event.target_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if event.exclude_surface_id and conn.surface_id == event.exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n\n            # Queue the event\n            try:\n                conn.queue.put_nowait(event)\n                sent_count += 1\n            except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                logger.warning(f\"Queue full for connection {conn.connection_id}, dropping event\")\n\n        return sent_count\n\n    async def event_generator(self, connection: SSEConnection):\n        \"\"\"\n        Generate SSE events for a connection.\n\n        Yields formatted SSE messages.\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Send initial connection event\n            yield self._format_sse(\"connected\", {\n                \"connection_id\": connection.connection_id,\n                \"surface_id\": connection.surface_id,\n                \"session_id\": connection.session_id,\n            })\n\n            while True:\n                # Block on the next event, but no longer than the keepalive\n                # interval: if nothing arrives, emit a comment-line ping so the\n                # stream is never silent. A silent idle stream is indistinguish-\n                # able from a dead one to the browser — EventSource only fires\n                # ``onerror`` when there is I/O to fail — so without these pings\n                # a real network drop on an idle surface leaves it showing\n                # \"Connected\" until the OS TCP keepalive times out (minutes\n                # later). The ping is a comment line (``\": ping\\n\\n\"``), which\n                # EventSource ignores — it surfaces no message to the client.\n                try:\n                    event = await asyncio.wait_for(\n                        connection.queue.get(),\n                        timeout=KEEPALIVE_INTERVAL_SECONDS,\n                    )\n                except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                    yield \": ping\\n\\n\"\n                    continue\n\n                # Abrupt drop sentinel (see _DROP): end the stream WITHOUT a\n                # disconnect event so the browser EventSource onerrors + reconnects.\n                if event is _DROP:\n                    logger.info(f\"Drop sentinel on connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    return\n\n                connection.last_heartbeat = datetime.now().timestamp()\n\n                # Format and yield the event\n                payload = dict(event.data)\n                if event.rendered_html is not None:\n                    payload[\"rendered_html\"] = event.rendered_html\n                yield self._format_sse(event.event_type, payload)\n\n                # Special case: disconnect event ends the stream\n                if event.event_type == \"disconnect\":\n                    logger.info(f\"Disconnect event for connection {connection.connection_id}\")\n                    break\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"Connection {connection.connection_id} cancelled\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.unregister(connection.connection_id)\n\n    def _format_sse(self, event_type: str, data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format an event as SSE.\"\"\"\n        return f\"event: {event_type}\\ndata: {json.dumps(data)}\\n\\n\"\n\n    async def _cleanup_loop(self):\n        \"\"\"Periodically clean up dead connections.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await asyncio.sleep(30)  # Check every 30 seconds\n\n                now = datetime.now().timestamp()\n                timeout = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n                dead_connections = [\n                    cid for cid, conn in self.connections.items()\n                    if (now - conn.last_heartbeat) > timeout\n                ]\n\n                for cid in dead_connections:\n                    logger.info(f\"Cleaning up dead connection {cid}\")\n                    # Send disconnect event before removing\n                    try:\n                        conn = self.connections[cid]\n                        conn.queue.put_nowait(SSEEvent(\n                            event_type=\"disconnect\",\n                            data={\"reason\": \"timeout\"},\n                        ))\n                    except asyncio.QueueFull:\n                        pass\n                    self.unregister(cid)\n\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except    start = time.time()\n244\t        try:\n245\t            data = await executor(context)\n246\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n247\t\n248\t            return )\n254\t\n255\t        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n256\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n257\t            return SourceResult(\n258\t                source=spec.source,\n259\t                status=\"timeout\",\n260\t                data={},\n261\t                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n262\t                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n263\t            )\n264\t\n265\t        except Exception as e:\n266\t            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n267\t            return SourceResult(\n268\t       )\n274\t\n275\t    # Source executors\n276\t\n277\t    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n278\t        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n279\t resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n406\t        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n407\t        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n408\t        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n409\t        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n410\t        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n411\t        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n412\t\n413\t        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n414\t        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n415\t        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n416\t        carrying the reason. Never a silent wrong-instance query.\n417\t        \"\"\"\n418\t        app_name = context.app_name or context.project_slug\n419\t        if not app_name:\n420\t            return {\"error\": \"No application name specified\"}\n421\t\n422\t        resolution = resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)\n423\t        if not resolution.satisfiable:\n424\t            raise ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(\n425\t                resolution.reason or \"ArgoCD endpoint unresolvable\",\n426\t                cluster=context.cluster,\n427\t            )\n428\t\n429\t        proxy = resolution.argocd_api\n430\t        try:\n431\t            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0, verify=False) as client:\n432\t                resp = await client.get(\n433\t                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/applications\",\n434\t                    params={\"name\": app_name}\n435\t                )\n436\t                resp.raise_for_status()\n437\t                apps_data = resp.json()\n438\t\n439\t                if not apps_data.get(\"items\"):\n440\t                    return {\"error\": f\"Application not found: {app_name}\"}\n441\t\n442\t                app = apps_data[\"items\"][0]\n443\t                status = app.get(\"status\", {})\n444\t                operation = app.get(\"operation\", {})\n445\t\n446\t                return {\n447\t                    \"name\": app_name,\n448\t                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n449\t                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n450\t                    \"revision\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"revision\", \"\"),\n451\t                    \"operation\": operation.get(\"operation\", {}),\n452\t                    \"created_at\": status.get(\"operationState\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n453\t                }\n454\t\n455\t        except Exception as e:\n456\t            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n457\t\n458\t    def _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n459\t        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n460\t        if context.ssh_target:\n461\t            return [\"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n462\t                    context.ssh_target, \" \".join(args)]\n463\t        return list(args)\n464\t\n465\t    async def _fetch_git_log(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n466\t        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n467\t        repo_path =              commits.append({\n494\t                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n495\t                            \"message\": parts[1],\n496\t                            \"author\": )\n613\t\n614\t                stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n615\t\n616\t                if proc.returncode == 0:\n617\t                    import json as _json\n618\t orchestrator: coordinates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n\nRuns all fetch sources concurrently, provides incremental results via callbacks,\ntracks coverage, and surfaces caveats for failed sources.\n\nThis is the canonical fetch implementation, consolidating the command matrix,\nconcurrent execution, and coverage tracking into a single module.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport get_fetch_commands,\n    get_required_sources,\n    SourceResult,\n)\nfrom .clusters import ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable, resolve_argocd_endpoint\n\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# Type alias for the streaming callback\n# Called when a source completes: (source, result) -> None\nStreamCallback = Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]\n\n\nclass FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"\n    Fetch strand: concurrent data fetcher with streaming support.\n\n    Executes all fetch commands for an intent concurrently, streams partial\n    results as they arrive, and provides comprehensive coverage tracking.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self):\n        self._source_executors = {\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_PODS: self._fetch_kubectl_pods,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS: self._fetch_kubectl_deployments,\n            FetchSource.KUBECTL_WORKFLOWS: self._fetch_kubectl_workflows,\n            FetchSource.ARGOCD_APP: self._fetch_argocd_app,\n            FetchSource.GIT_LOG: self._fetch_git_log,\n            FetchSource.GIT_STATUS: self._fetch_git_status,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_LIST: self._fetch_bead_list,\n            FetchSource.BEAD_DETAILS:        FetchSource.EVENTS: self._fetch_events,\n            FetchSource.SESSION_STATE: self._fetch_session_state,\n            FetchSource.TOPIC_CONTEXT: self._fetch_topic_context,\n            FetchSource.REMINDERS: self._fetch_reminders,\n            FetchSource.FS_EXPLORE: self._fetch_fs_explore,\n            FetchSource.FS_README: self._fetch_fs_readme,\n            FetchSource.FS_HOME: self._fetch_fs_home,\n        }\n\n    async def fetch(\n        self,\n        request: FetchRequest,\n        on_partial_result: Optional[Callable[[FetchSource, SourceResult], None]] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute fetch request with optional streaming callback.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request to execute\n            on_partial_result: Optional callback for streaming partial results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with coverage information\n        \"\"\"\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Get fetch commands for this intent type\n        command_specs = get_fetch_commands(request.intent_type)\n        required_sources = get_required_sources(request.intent_type)\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching {len(command_specs)} sources for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Create tasks for concurrent execution\n        tasks = []\n        for spec in command_specs:\n            task = asyncio.create_task(\n                self._execute_source(spec, request.context),\n                name=f\"fetch_{spec.source.value}_{request.intent_id[:8]}\",\n            )\n            # Get effective timeout from config file or spec default\n            effective_timeout = get_effective_timeout(spec, request.context.project_slug)\n            tasks.append((spec.source, spec.required, effective_timeout, task))\n\n        # Wait for all tasks to complete with per-source timeout enforcement\n        # We use asyncio.wait with ALL_COMPLETED to detect window close:\n        # - All tasks run concurrently\n        # - Each task is wrapped to handle its own timeout\n        # - As tasks complete, we process them immediately\n        # - Window closes when ALL tasks reach terminal state (success/timeout/error)\n        sources = {}\n        succeeded = []\n        timed_out = []\n        failed = []\n        skipped = []\n        caveats = []\n\n        # Wrap each task with its timeout and error handling\n        # This allows true concurrent waiting with per-source timeouts\n        async def execute_with_timeout(\n            source: FetchSource,\n            required: bool,\n            timeout: int | float,\n            task: asyncio.Task\n        ) -> tuple[FetchSource, bool, SourceResult]:\n            \"\"\"Execute a task with timeout, returning (source, required, result).\"\"\"\n            try:\n                result = await asyncio.wait_for(task, timeout=timeout)\n                return source, required, result\n            except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n                logger.warning(f\"Source {source.value} timed out after {timeout}s\")\n                result = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"timeout\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=f\"Timed out after {timeout}s\",\n                    duration_ms=int(timeout * 1000),\n                )\n                return source, required, result\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error fetching {source.value}: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                result = SourceResult(\n                    source=source,\n                    status=\"error\",\n                    data={},\n                    error=str(e),\n                    duration_ms=0,\n                )\n                return source, required, result\n\n        # Create wrapped tasks for concurrent execution\n        wrapped_tasks = [\n            execute_with_timeout(source, required, timeout, task)\n            for source, required, timeout, task in tasks\n        ]\n\n        # Wait for all wrapped tasks to complete concurrently\n        # This is the WINDOW CLOSE detection: we wait until ALL tasks finish\n        completed_results = await asyncio.gather(*wrapped_tasks)\n\n        # Process results as they arrive (maintaining completion order)\n        for source, required, result in completed_results:\n            # Stream partial result immediately (progressive window fill)\n            if on_partial_result:\n                on_partial_result(source, result)\n\n            sources[source] = result\n\n            if result.status == \"success\":\n                succeeded.append(source)\n                # Check if the source data contains a caveat (e.g., fallback path with narrower scope)\n                if isinstance(result.data, dict) and \"caveat\" in result.data:\n                    caveats.append(result.data[\"caveat\"])\n            elif result.status == \"timeout\":\n                timed_out.append(source)\n                caveats.append(f\"{source.value} timed out\")\n            elif result.status == \"error\":\n                failed.append(source)\n                if required:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Required source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n                else:\n                    caveats.append(f\"Optional source {source.value} failed: {result.error}\")\n\n        # Build coverage report\n        coverage = FetchCoverage(\n            total_sources=len(command_specs),\n            succeeded=succeeded,\n            timed_out=timed_out,\n            failed=failed,\n            skipped=skipped,\n        )\n\n        total_duration_ms = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n\n        result = FetchResult(\n            intent_id=request.intent_id,\n            intent_type=request.intent_type,\n            sources=sources,\n            coverage=coverage,\n            total_duration_ms=total_duration_ms,\n            caveats=caveats if caveats else None,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{len(succeeded)}/{coverage.total_sources} succeeded, \"\n            f\"{len(timed_out)} timed out, {len(failed)} failed\"\n        )\n\n        # Detect if ALL sources failed - this is a terminal failure condition\n        # The caller (intent router) should broadcast all_sources_failed event\n        if len(succeeded) == 0 and coverage.total_sources > 0:\n            logger.error(\n                f\"All fetch sources failed for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n                f\"{len(failed)} failed, {len(timed_out)} timed out\"\n            )\n            # Mark result with terminal failure flag\n            result.terminal_failure = \"all_sources_failed\"\n\n        return result\n\n    async def _execute_source(\n        self,\n        spec,\n        context: FetchContext,\n    ) -> SourceResult:\n        \"\"\"Execute a single fetch source.\"\"\"\n        executor = self._source_executors.get(spec.source)\n        if not executor:\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"No executor for source {spec.source.value}\",\n                duration_ms=0,\n            )\n\n        start = time.time()\n        try:\n            data = await executor(context)\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"success\",\n                data=data,\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"timeout\",\n                data={},\n                error=f\"Timed out after {spec.timeout_seconds}s\",\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            duration_ms = int((time.time() - start) * 1000)\n            return SourceResult(\n                source=spec.source,\n                status=\"error\",\n                data={},\n                error=str(e),\n                duration_ms=duration_ms,\n            )\n\n    # Source executors\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_pods(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch pod status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n\n        if not namespace:\n            # Try to derive from project_slug\n            if context.project_slug:\n                namespace = context.project_slug.replace(\"-\", \"\")\n            else:\n                return {\"error\": \"No namespace specified\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/{namespace}/pods\"\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                pods_data = resp.json()\n\n                pods = []\n                for pod in pods_data.get(\"items\", []):\n                    pod_name =    pods.append({\n                        \"name\": pod_name,\n                        \"phase\": phase,\n                        \"ready\": ready,\n                        \"restarts\": restarts,\n                    })\n\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"pods\": pods,\n                    \"pod_count\": len(pods),\n                    \"healthy_count\": sum(1 for p in pods if p[\"phase\"] == \"Running\"),\n                }\n\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                return {\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"error\": \"Namespace not found\",\n                    \"pods\": [],\n                }\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"pods\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_deployments(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch deployment status from kubernetes.\"\"\"\n        proxy = context.proxy\n        namespace = context.namespace\n        deployment = context.deployment or context.app_name\n\n        if not namespace or not deployment:\n            return {\"error\": \"Missing namespace or deployment name\"}\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await resp.json()\n\n                status = deploy_data.get(\"status\", {})\n                spec = deploy_data.get(\"spec\", {})\n\n                return {\n                    \"name\": deployment,\n                    \"namespace\": namespace,\n                    \"replicas\": spec.get(\"replicas\", 0),\n                    \"ready_replicas\": status.get(\"readyReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"available_replicas\": status.get(\"availableReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"updated_replicas\": status.get(\"updatedReplicas\", 0),\n                    \"conditions\": status.get(\"conditions\", []),\n                }\n\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 404:\n                return {\"error\": f\"Deployment not found: {deployment}\"}\n            raise\n\n    async def _fetch_kubectl_workflows(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch Argo Workflow status from CI cluster.\"\"\"\n        proxy = KUBECTL_PROXIES.get(\"iad-ci\", context.proxy)\n        project_slug = context.project_slug\n\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/namespaces/argo-workflows/workflows\",\n                    params={\"labelSelector\": f\"project={project_slug}\"} if project_slug else None\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                workflows_data = resp.json()\n\n                workflows = []\n                for wf in _fetch_argocd_app(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch ArgoCD application status.\n\n        The ArgoCD endpoint is resolved from the project's ``cluster`` via\n        config/clusters.yaml (see src/fetch/clusters.py): there is no single\n        ArgoCD API, and querying the wrong instance returns not-found —\n        indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\". An unmapped cluster, or one\n        mapped to an ``access`` mode the strand cannot satisfy (it holds no\n        ArgoCD credentials, so only ``read-only-proxy`` is consumable), raises\n        ``ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable`` *before* any HTTP call.\n\n        The exception is raised outside the httpx try/except so the resolution\n        ``reason`` propagates cleanly: ``_execute_source`` buckets it as a\n        failed source and the fetch loop emits a ``fetch_coverage`` caveat\n        carrying the reason. Never a silent wrong-instance query.\n        \"\"\"\n        app_name = context.app_name or context.project_slug\n        if not app_name:\n            return {\"error\": \"No application name specified\"}\n\n        resolution = resolve_argocd_endpoint(context.cluster)\n        if not resolution.satisfiable:\n            raise ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(\n                resolution.reason or \"ArgoCD endpoint unresolvable\",\n                cluster=context.cluster,\n            )\n\n        proxy = resolution.argocd_api\n        try:\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=10.0, verify=False) as client:\n                resp = await client.get(\n                    f\"{proxy}/api/v1/applications\",\n                    params={\"name\": app_name}\n                )\n                resp.raise_for_status()\n                apps_data = resp.json()\n\n                if not apps_data.get(\"items\"):\n                    return {\"error\": f\"Application not found: {app_name}\"}\n\n                app = apps_data[\"items\"][0]\n                status = app.get(\"status\", {})\n                operation = app.get(\"operation\", {})\n\n                return {\n                    \"name\": app_name,\n                    \"sync_status\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"health_status\": status.get(\"health\", {}).get(\"status\", \"Unknown\"),\n                    \"revision\": status.get(\"sync\", {}).get(\"revision\", \"\"),\n                    \"operation\": operation.get(\"operation\", {}),\n                    \"created_at\": status.get(\"operationState\", {}).get(\"startedAt\", \"\"),\n                }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n    def _make_cmd(self, context: FetchContext, *args: str) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Wrap a command with SSH if the repo is remote.\"\"\"\n        if context.ssh_target:\n            return [\"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target, \" \".join(args)]\n        return list(args)\n\n    async def _fetch_git_log(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git log for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Fetch commits with author and date\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"log\", \"-10\",\n                                \"--pretty=format:%h|%s|%an|%ar\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            commits = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line:\n                    parts = line.split(\"|\", 3)\n                    if len(parts) == 4:\n                        commits.append({\n                            \"hash\": parts[0],\n                            \"message\": parts[1],\n                            \"author\": parts[2],\n                            \"date\": parts[3],\n                        })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                *self._make_cmd(context, \"git\", \"-C\", repo_path, \"branch\", \"--show-current\"),\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"commits\": commits,\n                \"count\": len(commits),\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"commits\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_git_status(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Fetch git status for a project (local or remote via SSH).\"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        if not repo_path:\n            return {\"error\": \"No repo path resolved\"}\n        if not context.ssh_target and not Path(repo_path).exists():\n            return {\"error\": f\"Repo path not found: {repo_path}\"}\n\n        try:\n            # Get status output\n            proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"status\", \"--short\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n\n            stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n            if proc.returncode != 0:\n                return {\"error\": stderr.decode()}\n\n            # Parse status output - format: XY filename\n            changed_files = []\n            for line in stdout.decode().strip().split(\"\\n\"):\n                if line and len(line) >= 3:\n                    status_code = line[:2]\n                    filename = line[3:]\n                    changed_files.append({\n                        \"status\": status_code,\n                        \"file\": filename,\n                    })\n\n            # Get current branch\n            branch_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"branch\", \"--show-current\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            branch_stdout, _ = await branch_proc.communicate()\n            branch = branch_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            # Get last commit\n            log_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                \"git\",\n                \"-C\", repo_path,\n                \"log\", \"-1\", \"--oneline\",\n                stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n            )\n            log_stdout, _ = await log_proc.communicate()\n            last_commit = log_stdout.decode().strip()\n\n            return {\n                \"repo\": repo_path,\n                \"branch\": branch,\n                \"last_commit\": last_commit,\n                \"changed_files\": changed_files,\n                \"count\": len(changed_files),\n                \"has_changes\": len(changed_files) > 0,\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e), \"changed_files\": []}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_list(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch bead list from the appropriate workspace.\n\n        Per plan Beads-Workspace Scoping:\n        - Primary path: run `bf list --status open` (NO --project filter) inside the\n          project's repo_path checkout when it has a .beads/ workspace\n        - Fallback (no repo_path/.beads): list from the adc workspace filtered\n          --project {slug}, with a fetch_coverage caveat naming the narrower scope\n        \"\"\"\n        repo_path = context.repo_path\n        project_slug = context.project_slug\n\n        # ADC workspace for fallback path (all aide-de-camp-originated beads live here)\n        adc_workspace = \"/home/coding/aide-de-camp\"\n\n        try:\n            if context.ssh_target:\n                # Remote: check for .beads and run bf via SSH in one shot\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target,\n                    f\"cd {repo_path} && [ -f .beads/issues.jsonl ] && bf list --status=open --limit=50 2>&1 || echo '{{\\\"error\\\":\\\"no beads workspace\\\"}}'\",\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                )\n\n                stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n                if proc.returncode == 0:\n                    import json as _json\n                    try:\n                        beads = _json.loads(stdout.decode())\n                    except Exception:\n                        beads = []\n                    return {\n                        \"project\": project_slug,\n                        \"repo\": context.display_path if hasattr(context, \"display_path\") else repo_path,\n                        \"host\": context.host_alias,\n                        \"beads\": beads,\n                        \"count\": len(beads) if isinstance(beads, list) else 0,\n                        \"scope\": \"project_workspace\",  # Primary path\n                    }\n                else:\n                    error_output = stderr.decode().strip() or stdout.decode().strip()\n                    if \"no beads workspace\" in error_output or \"No such file\" in error_output:\n                        # Fallback path: no local .beads workspace at remote\n                        # Use adc workspace with --project filter\n                        return await self._fetch_bead_list_from_adc(project_slug, repo_path, context, remote_ssh=context.ssh_target)\n                    return {\"error\": error_output or \"bf list returned non-zero\"}\n            else:\n                # Local: check for .beads workspace first\n                from pathlib import Path as _Path\n\n                if repo_path and (_Path(repo_path) / \".beads\" / \"issues.jsonl\").exists():\n                    # Primary path: project has its own .beads/ workspace\n                    # Run bf list without --project filter (a project's own workspace\n                    # doesn't tag its beads with the aide-de-camp slug)\n                    proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                        \"bf\", \"list\", \"--status=open\", \"--limit=50\",\n                        stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                        stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                        cwd=repo_path,\n                    )\n\n                    stdout, stderr = await proc.communicate()\n\n                    if proc.returncode == 0:\n                        import json as _json\n                        try:\n                            beads = _json.loads(stdout.decode())\n                        except Exception:\n                            beads = []\n                        return {\n                            \"project\": project_slug,\n                            \"repo\": repo_path,\n                            \"beads\": beads,\n                            \"count\": len(beads) if isinstance(beads, list) else 0,\n                            \"scope\": \"project_workspace\",  # Primary path\n                        }\n                    else:\n                        return {\"error\": stderr.decode().strip() or \"bf list returned non-zero\"}\n                else:\n                    # Fallback path: no local .beads workspace or no repo_path\n                    # Use adc workspace with --project filter\n                    return await self._fetch_bead_list_from_adc(project_slug, repo_path, context)\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return {\"error\": \"bf CLI not found in PATH\"}\n        except Exception as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n    async def _fetch_bead_list_from_adc(\n        self, project_slug: str | None, repo_path: str | None, context: FetchContext, remote_ssh: str | None = None\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch bead list from adc workspace with --project filter (fallback path).\n\n        Per plan Beads-Workspace Scoping fallback:\n        \"showing        context.ssh_target,\n                    f\"ls -la {context.repo_path} 2>&1\",\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                )\n                stdout, _ = await proc.communicate()\n                return {\n                    \"path\": f\"{context.ssh_target}:{context.repo_path}\",\n                    \"host\": \"name\": entry.name,\n                    \"type\": \"dir\" if entry.is_dir() else \"file\",\n                    \"size\": entry.stat().st_size if entry.is_file() else None,\n                })\n        except PermissionError as e:\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n        return {\"path\": str(explore_path), \"items\": items, \"count\": len(items)}\n\n    async def _fetch_fs_readme(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Read the README from the resolved repo directory (local or remote).\"\"\"\n        if not context.repo_path:\n            return 2>/dev/null\"\n                    for name in (\"README.md\", \"README.txt\", \"README.rst\", \"README\")\n                )\n                proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(\n                    \"ssh\", \"-o\", \"ConnectTimeout=8\", \"-o\", \"BatchMode=yes\",\n                    context.ssh_target, script,\n                    stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                    stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,\n                )\n                stdout, _ = await proc.communicate()\n                content = stdout.decode(errors=\"replace\")[:4000]\n                if {context.host_alias}:{context.repo_path}\"}\n            except Exception as e:\n                return {\"error\": str(e)}\n\n        from pathlib import Path as _Path\n        repo = str(e)}\n\n        return {\"error\": f\"No README found in {context.repo_path}\"}\n\n    async def _fetch_fs_home(self, context: FetchContext) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Overview of /home/coding/ — all             \"home\": \"/home/coding\",\n                \"total_repos\": summary[\"total_repos\"],\n                \"beaded_repos\": summary[\"beaded_repos\"],\n                \"repos\": entries,\n            }\n\n        # Fallback: direct scan if registry not ready\n        from pathlib import Path as _Path\n        home = _Path(\"/home/coding\")\n        repos = []\n        for d in     \"path\": str(d),\n                    \"has_beads\": (d / \".beads\").is_dir(),\n                })\n        return {\"home\": str(home), \"repos\": repos, \"total_repos\": len(repos)}\n\n    def _get_pod_ready(self, pod_data: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get pod ready status (e.g., '1/1').\"\"\"\n        container_statuses = pod_data.get(\"status\", {}).get(\"containerStatuses\", [])\n        if not container_statuses:\n            return \"0/0\"\n\n        ready_count = sum(1 for cs in container_statuses if cs.get(\"ready\", False))\n        total_count = len(container_statuses)\n\n        return f\"{ready_count}/{total_count}\"\n\n\nclass FetchOrchestrator:\n    \"\"\"\n    Orchestrates concurrent fetch execution with streaming support.\n\n    This is a convenience wrapper around FetchStrand that provides the same interface.\n    The actual fetch execution is handled by the FetchStrand instance.\n\n    Features:\n    - Concurrent execution of all fetch sources\n    - Streaming callbacks as sources complete\n    - Per-source timeout enforcement\n    - Coverage tracking (succeeded, timed_out, failed, skipped)\n    - Caveat generation for failed sources\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, fetch_strand: Optional[FetchStrand] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize the orchestrator.\n\n        Args:\n            fetch_strand: FetchStrand instance (uses global if not provided)\n        \"\"\"\n        if fetch_strand is None:\n            self._strand = FetchStrand()\n        else:\n            self._strand = fetch_strand\n\n    async def execute_fetch(\n        self,\n        request: FetchRequest,\n        stream_callback: Optional[StreamCallback] = None,\n    ) -> FetchResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Execute a fetch request with concurrent source execution.\n\n        Args:\n            request: The fetch request with intent type and context\n            stream_callback: Optional callback for incremental results\n\n        Returns:\n            FetchResult with all source results and coverage info\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Starting fetch for intent {request.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type={request.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        # Delegate to FetchStrand which handles:\n        # - Concurrent execution\n        # - Per-source timeouts\n        # - Streaming callbacks\n        # - Coverage tracking\n        result = await self._strand.fetch(\n            request=request,\n            on_partial_result=stream_callback,\n        )\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetch complete for intent {request.intent_id}: \"\n            f\"{result.coverage.success_rate:.1%} success, \"\n            f\"{result.total_duration_ms}ms\"\n        )\n\n        return result\n\n\n# Global orchestrator instance\n_orchestrator: Optional[FetchOrchestrator] = None\n\n\ndef get_orchestrator() -> FetchOrchestrator:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global orchestrator instance.\"\"\"\n    global _orchestrator\n    if _orchestrator is None:\n        _orchestrator = FetchOrchestrator()\n    return _orchestrator\n\n\nasync def execute_fetch(\n    request: FetchRequest,\n    stream_callback: Optional[StreamCallback] = None,\n) -> FetchResult:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience function to execute a fetch request.\n\n    Args:\n        request: The fetch request\n        stream_callback: Optional callback for incremental results (receives source, result)\n\n    Returns:\n        FetchResult with all source results\n    \"\"\"\n    orchestrator = get_orchestrator()\n    return await orchestrator.execute_fetch(request, stream_callback)\n\n\n# Global fetch strand instance\n_fetch_strand: Optional[FetchStrand] = None\n\n\ndef get_fetch_strand() -> FetchStrand:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global fetch strand instance.\"\"\"\n    global _fetch_strand\n    if _fetch_strand is None:\n        _fetch_strand = FetchStrand()\n    return        }\n1050\t\n1051\t        async function registerSurface() {\n1052\t            try {\n1053\t                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n1054\t The welcome card is\n1111\t                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n1112\t                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n1113\t                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n1114\t                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n1115\t                const projects = cards.length ? seenCardIds.add(cardId);\n1131\t\n1132\t                    const existingCard = existingCards.get(cardId);\n1133\t                    if (existingCard) {\n1134\t                        // In-place                  } else {\n1138\t                        // Create new card\n1139\t                        const newCard = createTopicCard(cardData);\n1140\t                        container.appendChild(newCard);\n1141\t                    }\n1142\t                });\n1143\t\n1144\t                // Remove cards that are no longer in the new set (excluding welcome/error/stuck/failed cards)\n1145\t                existingCards.forEach((card, cardId) => {\n1146\t                    if (!seenCardIds.has(cardId) && !card.dataset.builtin) {\n1147\t                        card.remove();\n1148\t                    }\n1149\t                });\n1150\t\n1151\t                // Handle welcome card for empty state\n1152\t                if (cards.length === 0)              console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n1168\t            }\n1169\t        }\n1170\t\n1171\t        function connectSSE() {\n1172\t            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', };\n1181\t\n1182\t            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n1183\t                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n1184\t                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected',       threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n1222\t                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n1223\t                } else {\n1224\t threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n1227\t                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n1228\t                }\n1229\t\n1230\t                // No        // Find the pending thread card by thread_id\n1245\t                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + threadId + '\"]');\n1246\t                if (!threadCard)         return;\n1253\t                }\n1254\t                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n1255\t                    console.warn('thread_progress: _setProgress is not a });\n1264\t\n1265\t            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n1266\t            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n1267\t                const data = });\n1527\t\n1528\t                if (!resp.ok) {\n1529\t                    var errText = await resp.text();\n1530\t                    console.error('Dispatch error:', errText);\n1531\t element\n1747\t        function buildSelector(el) {\n1748\t            if (!el || el === document.body) return 'body';\n1749\t            var parts = [];\n1750\t            var cur = el;\n1751\t            while (cur && cur !== document.body && cur !== document.documentElement) {\n1752\t                if (cur.id) { parts.unshift('#' + cur.id); break; }\n1753\t                var tag =
