           color: #f8fafc;\n        }\n\n        .approval-reject:hover:not(:disabled) {\n            background: #dc2626;\n            box-shadow: 0 4px 8px rgba(239, 68, 68, 0.3);\n            transform: translateY(-1px);\n        }\n\n        .approval-reject:disabled {\n            background: #7f1d1d;\n            cursor: not-allowed;\n            opacity: 0.5;\n        }\n    </style>\n</head>\n<body>\n    <div class=\"container\">\n        <header>\n            <div style=\"display:flex;align-items:baseline;gap:8px\">\n                <h1>ADC Canvas</h1>\n                <span id=\"versionBadge\" style=\"font-size:12px;color:#475569;font-weight:500;font-family:'Monaco','Menlo',monospace\"></span>\n            </div>\n            <div class=\"status\">\n                <span class=\"status-dot\" id=\"statusDot\"></span>\n                <span id=\"statusText\">Connecting...</span>\n            </div>\n        </header>\n\n        <div class=\"workload-summary\" id=\"workloadSummary\"></div>\n\n        <div class=\"input-bar\">\n            <textarea\n                id=\"inputTextarea\"\n                class=\"input-textarea\"\n                placeholder=\"Ask anything — status of the options pipeline, what's in flight, open beads…\"\n                rows=\"1\"\n            ></textarea>\n            <button class=\"btn-mic\" id=\"btnMic\" title=\"Voice input\">\n                <svg width=\"20\" height=\"20\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <path d=\"M12 2a3 3 0 0 0-3 3v7a3 3 0 0 0 6 0V5a3 3 0 0 0-3-3z\"/>\n                    <path d=\"M19 10v2a7 7 0 0 1-14 0v-2\"/>\n                    <line x1=\"12\" y1=\"19\" x2=\"12\" y2=\"23\"/>\n                    <line x1=\"8\" y1=\"23\" x2=\"16\" y2=\"23\"/>\n                </svg>\n            </button>\n            <button class=\"btn-send\" id=\"btnSend\" title=\"Send\" disabled>\n                <svg width=\"18\" height=\"18\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\">\n                    <line x1=\"22\" y1=\"2\" x2=\"11\" y2=\"13\"/>\n                    <polygon points=\"22 2 15 22 11 13 2 9 22 2\"/>\n                </svg>\n            </button>\n        </div>\n\n        <div id=\"topicsContainer\" class=\"topics-grid\">\n            <div class=\"loading\">\n                <div class=\"spinner\"></div>\n                <span>Loading topics...</span>\n            </div>\n        </div>\n    </div>\n\n    <!-- Render helpers (createTopicCard + friends) — loaded before the app\n         script so they're available as globals and unit-testable headlessly. -->\n    <script src=\"/canvas.js\"></script>\n    <script>\n        // Session and surface IDs from URL params or generate new\n        const urlParams = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);\n        const sessionId = urlParams.get('session_id') || generateId();\n        const surfaceType = 'canvas';\n\n        let surfaceId = null;\n        let eventSource = null;\n\n        // Track pending placeholders so we can find/replace them later.\n        // Key: pendingId (utterance_id), Value: placeholder HTMLElement.\n        var pendingPlaceholders = {};\n\n        // Update URL with session_id for reload persistence\n        if (!urlParams.get('session_id')) {\n            urlParams.set('session_id', sessionId);\n            window.history.replaceState({}, '', `${window.location.pathname}?${urlParams}`);\n        }\n\n        function generateId() {\n            return 'adc-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 16);\n        }\n\n        // createTopicCard / escapeHtml / formatStaleness / getStalenessLevel live\n        // in /canvas.js (loaded below as a blocking <script src> before this\n        // block), so they are available as globals here and unit-testable\n        // headlessly. See src/canvas/canvas.js.\n\n        function updateConnectionStatus(status, text) {\n            const dot = document.getElementById('statusDot');\n            const statusText = document.getElementById('statusText');\n\n            dot.className = 'status-dot ' + status;\n            statusText.textContent = text;\n        }\n\n        function showWorkloadSummary(summary) {\n            const el = document.getElementById('workloadSummary');\n            const parts = [];\n\n            if (summary.pending_intents > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.pending_intents}</strong> pending intents`);\n            }\n            if (summary.new_results > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.new_results}</strong> new results`);\n            }\n            if (summary.unresolved_exceptions > 0) {\n                parts.push(`<strong>${summary.unresolved_exceptions}</strong> unresolved exceptions`);\n            }\n\n            if (parts.length > 0) {\n                el.innerHTML = '📊 While you were away: ' + parts.join(', ');\n                el.classList.add('show');\n            } else {\n                el.classList.remove('show');\n            }\n        }\n\n        async function registerSurface() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/register', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        session_id: sessionId,\n                        surface_type: surfaceType\n                    })\n                });\n\n                if (!response.ok) throw new Error('Failed to register surface');\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                surfaceId = data.surface_id;\n                return surfaceId;\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error registering surface:', error);\n                throw error;\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Registry project list for the first-run welcome card\n        // (config/registry.yaml → GET /api/v1/registry). DB/result independent —\n        // the welcome card renders even against an empty component store. Cached\n        // after the first fetch so the per-SSE-event reloads don't refetch it.\n        let welcomeProjects = null;\n\n        async function loadWelcomeProjects() {\n            if (welcomeProjects) return welcomeProjects;\n            try {\n                const resp = await fetch('/api/v1/registry');\n                if (resp.ok) {\n                    const data = await resp.json();\n                    welcomeProjects = (data && data.projects) || [];\n                    return welcomeProjects;\n                }\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading registry for welcome card:', error);\n            }\n            // Graceful fallback: an empty list still renders the welcome card\n            // (with default example utterances), so a wedged registry endpoint\n            // never leaves the canvas bare.\n            welcomeProjects = [];\n            return welcomeProjects;\n        }\n\n        async function loadTopics() {\n            try {\n                const response = await fetch(`/api/v1/sessions/${sessionId}/topics`);\n                if (!response.ok) return;\n\n                const data = await response.json();\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n                const cards = data.cards || [];\n\n                // Fresh session (zero topics): show the first-run welcome card\n                // instead of the bare empty-state. Its project list + example\n                // utterances come from the registry (never the DB), so we only\n                // need to fetch it on the zero-card branch. The welcome card is\n                // dropped — not duplicated — the moment the first real result\n                // lands: the next SSE result_created → loadTopics() reload\n                // yields a non-empty card set, so buildContainerChildren()\n                // returns topic cards only (clearing the welcome card).\n                const projects = cards.length ? [] : await loadWelcomeProjects();\n\n                // Build map of existing cards by card_id for in-place updates\n                const existingCards = new Map();\n                container.querySelectorAll('[data-card-id]').forEach(card => {\n                    const cardId = card.dataset.cardId;\n                    if (cardId) existingCards.set(cardId, card);\n                });\n\n                // Track which card_ids from the new set we've seen\n                const seenCardIds = new Set();\n\n                // Update or create cards\n                cards.forEach(cardData => {\n                    const cardId = cardData.card_id;\n                    seenCardIds.add(cardId);\n\n                    const existingCard = existingCards.get(cardId);\n                    if (existingCard) {\n                        // In-place update: replace the existing card with new content\n                        const newCard = createTopicCard(cardData);\n                        existingCard.replaceWith(newCard);\n                    } else {\n                        // Create new card\n                        const newCard = createTopicCard(cardData);\n                        container.appendChild(newCard);\n                    }\n                });\n\n                // Remove cards that are no longer in the new set (excluding welcome/error/stuck/failed cards)\n                existingCards.forEach((card, cardId) => {\n                    if (!seenCardIds.has(cardId) && !card.dataset.builtin) {\n                        card.remove();\n                    }\n                });\n\n                // Handle welcome card for empty state\n                if (cards.length === 0) {\n                    // Remove any existing welcome card\n                    const existingWelcome = container.querySelector('[data-builtin=\"welcome\"]');\n                    if (existingWelcome) existingWelcome.remove();\n\n                    // Add welcome card\n                    const welcomeCard = createWelcomeCard(projects);\n                    container.appendChild(welcomeCard);\n                } else {\n                    // Remove welcome card if we have real cards\n                    const welcomeCard = container.querySelector('[data-builtin=\"welcome\"]');\n                    if (welcomeCard) welcomeCard.remove();\n                }\n\n            } catch (error) {\n                console.error('Error loading topics:', error);\n            }\n        }\n\n        function connectSSE() {\n            updateConnectionStatus('connecting', 'Connecting...');\n\n            const url = `/api/v1/sse?surface_id=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceId)}&session_id=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}&surface_type=${encodeURIComponent(surfaceType)}`;\n            eventSource = new EventSource(url);\n\n            eventSource.onopen = () => {\n                updateConnectionStatus('', 'Connected');\n                loadTopics();\n            };\n\n            eventSource.onerror = (error) => {\n                console.error('SSE error:', error);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n            };\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('connected', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('SSE connected:', data);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('workload_summary', (event) => {\n                const summary = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                showWorkloadSummary(summary);\n            });\n\n            // Dispatch ack: backup path for intent_ids if the fetch response didn't\n            // include them (e.g., the response arrived before the intent was routed).\n            eventSource.addEventListener('dispatch_ack', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const utteranceId = data.utterance_id;\n                const intentIds = data.intent_ids;\n                const utterance = data.utterance;\n\n                if (!utteranceId || !intentIds || !Array.isArray(intentIds)) return;\n\n                // Check if there's an existing placeholder to split\n                const placeholder = pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n                const createdAt = placeholder\n                    ? parseInt(placeholder.dataset.createdAt, 10)\n                    : Date.now();\n\n                // Split into per-thread pending cards\n                const threadCards = splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds);\n\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                    // Replace the placeholder with the thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(frag, placeholder);\n                } else {\n                    // No placeholder (test scenario or race condition): prepend thread cards\n                    const frag = document.createDocumentFragment();\n                    threadCards.forEach(function (c) { frag.appendChild(c); });\n                    container.insertBefore(frag, container.firstChild);\n                }\n\n                // No longer track this placeholder\n                delete pendingPlaceholders[utteranceId];\n            });\n\n            // Thread-level progress event\n            eventSource.addEventListener('thread_progress', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const threadId = data.thread_id;\n\n                if (!threadId) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: missing thread_id in event data');\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by thread_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + threadId + '\"]');\n                if (!threadCard) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard not found for thread_id=' + threadId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: threadCard does not have pending-card class for thread_id=' + threadId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n                    console.warn('thread_progress: _setProgress is not a function');\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                // Pass progress message (e.g., '3/5 sources in') to _setProgress()\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n            });\n\n            // Per-source progress update for a thread\n            eventSource.addEventListener('progress_update', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                const completed = data.completed;\n                const total = data.total;\n\n                if (!intentId) return;\n\n                // Find the pending thread card by intent_id\n                const threadCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                if (!threadCard) {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard is null for intent_id=' + intentId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (!threadCard.classList.contains('pending-card')) {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: threadCard does not have pending-card class for intent_id=' + intentId);\n                    return;\n                }\n                if (typeof _setProgress !== 'function') {\n                    console.warn('progress_update: _setProgress is not a function');\n                    return;\n                }\n                _setProgress(threadCard, { completed: completed, total: total });\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('result_created', (event) => {\n                const result = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('New result:', result);\n\n                // Find and remove the pending card for this intent before reloading\n                const intentId = result.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        // Remove the pending card so it doesn't flicker when loadTopics() renders the real result\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                loadTopics(); // Reload topics to show new result\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('topic_updated', (event) => {\n                const topic = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Topic updated:', topic);\n                loadTopics();\n            });\n\n            // Task stuck event: render stuck card when a bead is fenced\n            eventSource.addEventListener('task_stuck', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Task stuck:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for stuck intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the stuck card\n                const stuckCard = createStuckCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend stuck card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(stuckCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            // Task failed event: render failed card on terminal failure\n            eventSource.addEventListener('task_failed', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Task failed:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for failed intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the failed card\n                const failedCard = createFailedCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend failed card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(failedCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            // Approval required event: render approval card for action beads\n            eventSource.addEventListener('approval_required', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Approval required:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector('[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]');\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for approval_required intent_id:', intentId);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the approval card\n                const approvalCard = createApprovalCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend approval card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(approvalCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            // Error event: render error card from degraded-state SSE error events\n            eventSource.addEventListener('error', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Error event:', data);\n\n                // Find and remove any pending card for this intent/pending before rendering error\n                const intentId = data.intent_id;\n                const pendingId = data.pending_id;\n                let selector = null;\n                if (intentId) {\n                    selector = '[data-pending-id=\"' + intentId + '\"]';\n                } else if (pendingId) {\n                    selector = '[data-pending-id=\"' + pendingId + '\"]';\n                }\n\n                if (selector) {\n                    const pendingCard = document.querySelector(selector);\n                    if (pendingCard) {\n                        pendingCard.remove();\n                        console.log('Removed pending card for error event:', selector);\n                    }\n                }\n\n                // Create and prepend the error card\n                const errorCard = createErrorCard(data);\n                const container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n                // Clear loading/empty state if present\n                if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                    container.innerHTML = '';\n                }\n\n                // Prepend error card (newest first)\n                container.insertBefore(errorCard, container.firstChild);\n            });\n\n            eventSource.addEventListener('disconnect', (event) => {\n                const data = JSON.parse(event.data);\n                console.log('Server disconnect:', data);\n                updateConnectionStatus('disconnected', 'Disconnected');\n                eventSource.close();\n            });\n        }\n\n        // Elapsed-time ticker for pending cards\n        // Every second, update the elapsed-time footer on all pending cards\n        // and apply aged treatment if they've exceeded the 30s threshold.\n        setInterval(function () {\n            var now = Date.now();\n            var pendingCards = document.querySelectorAll('.pending-card[data-pending-id]');\n            pendingCards.forEach(function (card) {\n                tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n                applyAgedTreatment(card, now);\n            });\n        }, 1000);\n\n        // Heartbeat to keep surface alive\n        setInterval(() => {\n            if (surfaceId) {\n                fetch('/api/v1/surfaces/heartbeat', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({\n                        surface_id: surfaceId,\n                        session_id: sessionId\n                    })\n                }).catch(console.error);\n            }\n        }, 30000); // Every 30 seconds\n\n        // ── Input bar ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n        const textarea = document.getElementById('inputTextarea');\n        const btnSend  = document.getElementById('btnSend');\n        const btnMic   = document.getElementById('btnMic');\n\n        // Auto-resize textarea\n        textarea.addEventListener('input', () => {\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            textarea.style.height = Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 160) + 'px';\n            btnSend.disabled = textarea.value.trim().length === 0;\n        });\n\n        // Send on Enter (Shift+Enter = newline)\n        textarea.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n            if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) {\n                e.preventDefault();\n                if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n            }\n        });\n\n        btnSend.addEventListener('click', () => {\n            if (!btnSend.disabled) dispatch(textarea.value.trim());\n        });\n\n        async function dispatch(utterance) {\n            if (!utterance) return;\n            textarea.value = '';\n            textarea.style.height = 'auto';\n            btnSend.disabled = true;\n\n            // Generate a client-side pending ID for the placeholder. We'll swap it\n            // for the server's utterance_id when the response arrives.\n            var pendingId = 'pending-' + Date.now() + '-' + Math.random().toString(36).substr(2, 8);\n            var createdAt = Date.now();\n\n            // Create the pending placeholder NOW, before the server round-trip,\n            // so a hung server still leaves a card on canvas to age.\n            var placeholder = createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId);\n            var container = document.getElementById('topicsContainer');\n\n            // Clear the empty-state/loading spinner if present\n            if (container.querySelector('.loading') || container.querySelector('.empty-state')) {\n                container.innerHTML = '';\n            }\n\n            // Prepend the placeholder (newest first)\n            container.insertBefore(placeholder, container.firstChild);\n\n            // Track it so we can find/replace it later\n            pendingPlaceholders[pendingId] = placeholder;\n\n            try {\n                var resp = await fetch('/dispatch', {\n                    method: 'POST',\n                    headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n                    body: JSON.stringify({ utterance, session_id: sessionId, surface_id: surfaceId, pending_id: pendingId }),\n                });\n\n                if (!resp.ok) {\n                    var errText = await resp.text();\n                    console.error('Dispatch error:', errText);\n                    // Try to parse as JSON for structured error\n                    var errData;\n                    try { errData = JSON.parse(errText); } catch (e) {}\n                    if (errData && errData.error_type) {\n                        // Replace placeholder with error card\n                        var errorCard = createErrorCard(errData);\n                        if (placeholder && placeholder.parentNode) {\n                            placeholder.parentNode.replaceChild(errorCard, placeholder);\n                        }\n                        delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n                    }\n                    return;\n                }\n\n                var data = await resp.json();\n                // Swap to the server's utterance_id for consistency\n                var utteranceId = data.utterance_id || pendingId;\n                placeholder.dataset.pendingId = utteranceId;\n\n                // Move the placeholder tracking to the new key\n                delete pendingPlaceholders[pendingId];\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_33d5f038691942799658620f","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t/*\n2\t * ADC Canvas — rendering helpers.\n3\t *\n4\t * Pure functions that turn a /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics card dict into a DOM\n5\t * topic-card element, and that build the four BUILT-IN card families the plan\n6\t * ships as fixed templates in this served frontend (Component Library →\n7\t * \"Built-in cards\"): (1) generic fallback, (2) first-run welcome card, (3)\n8\t * pending/ack cards (submit-time placeholder, per-thread pending with\n9\t * per-source progress, aged-pending treatment), and (4) the error/clarification\n10\t * family from Degraded-State UX. They are kept in this separate, loadable\n11\t * module (rather than inline in index.html) so the render contract can be\n12\t * verified headlessly — tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js and\n13\t * tests/e2e/canvas_builtin_runner.js load this exact file under a minimal DOM\n14\t * shim and assert the rendered HTML, with no browser or network required.\n15\t *\n16\t * ESCAPING CONTRACT (the render-path escaping contract, see plan UI-Regen Agent\n17\t * \"Escaping contract\"): every dynamic value — registry descriptions, utterance\n18\t * text, per-source names, error detail, elapsed strings, LLM/worker-authored\n19\t * free text — is interpolated through escapeHtml(), which sets it via a text\n20\t * node (textContent → the browser's own escaper). No dynamic value is ever\n21\t * spliced raw into innerHTML. The built-in builders below all go through the\n22\t * same escapeHtml() the topic card uses, so they are bound by the identical\n23\t * escaping contract.\n24\t *\n25\t * In the browser, index.html loads this via <script src=\"/canvas.js\"> before\n26\t * the inline app script, so these declarations are globals just like they were\n27\t * when they lived inline. In Node, the trailing export shim exposes them via\n28\t * module.exports for the DOM tests.\n29\t */\n30\t\n31\t// Node.js compatibility: ensure document is available in Node for headless tests\n32\t// When loaded in Node (module.exports exists), make the global document accessible\n33\t// to the el() helper function which uses document.createElement() and .createTextNode()\n34\tif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports && typeof document === 'undefined') {\n35\t    var document = global.document;\n36\t}\n37\t\n38\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n39\t// Tunables (exported so tests can assert against the same constants)\n40\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n41\t\n42\t// Hot-path intents flag at 30s pending (their budget is 3s) — see plan, The\n43\t// Async Path → Visible aging + Degraded-State UX aged-pending row. This flag is\n44\t// applied PURELY client-side from the local placeholder's creation time so it\n45\t// survives a hung/wedged server (no SSE dependency).\n46\tvar PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS = 30000;\n47\t\n48\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n49\t// Existing topic-card render helpers\n50\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n51\t\n52\tfunction formatStaleness(seconds) {\n53\t    if (seconds < 60) return 'just now';\n54\t    if (seconds < 3600) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 60)}m ago`;\n55\t    if (seconds < 86400) return `${Math.floor(seconds / 3600)}h ago`;\n56\t    return `${Math.floor(seconds / 86400)}d ago`;\n57\t}\n58\t\n59\tfunction getStalenessLevel(seconds) {\n60\t    if (seconds < 600) return 'fresh';      // 10 minutes\n61\t    if (seconds < 3600) return 'stale';     // 1 hour\n62\t    return 'very-stale';\n63\t}\n64\t\n65\t/**\n66\t * Build a topic card from topic data.\n67\t *\n68\t * @param {TopicCardData} cardData - Topic card data structure\n69\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .topic-card element (or fallback-card element if card_fallback is set)\n70\t */\n71\tfunction createTopicCard(cardData) {\n72\t    const topic = cardData.topic;\n73\t    const staleness = cardData.staleness;\n74\t    const latestResult = cardData.latest_result;\n75\t    const cardId = cardData.card_id;  // Unique (topic, result_type) identifier\n76\t\n77\t    // When the result has card_fallback=True, render the built-in generic fallback card\n78\t    // instead of the standard topic card. The fallback card is a complete card showing\n79\t    // result.data as a key/value grid (plan: Component Library → \"Built-in generic\n80\t    // fallback card\"). Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes for staleness\n81\t    // tracking and querying, but visually the card is the fallback presentation.\n82\t    if (latestResult && latestResult.card_fallback) {\n83\t        const fallbackCard = createFallbackCard(latestResult);\n84\t        // Preserve topic metadata for staleness tracking and querying\n85\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n86\t        fallbackCard.dataset.cardId = cardId;\n87\t        fallbackCard.dataset.topicType = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n88\t        fallbackCard.dataset.cardFallback = '1';\n89\t        return fallbackCard;\n90\t    }\n91\t\n92\t    // When the result has rendered_html (from hot-path component match), inject it directly.\n93\t    // The HTML is already escaped server-side (hot-path renderer's escaping contract),\n94\t    // so we inject it as-is. Topic metadata is preserved via data attributes.\n95\t    if (latestResult && latestResult.rendered_html) {\n96\t        const card = document.createElement('div');\n97\t        card.className = 'topic-card component-card';\n98\t        card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n99\t        card.dataset.cardId = cardId;\n100\t        card.dataset.componentId = latestResult.component_id || '';\n101\t\n102\t        const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n103\t        card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n104\t\n105\t        const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n106\t        card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n107\t\n108\t        // Build the full card HTML: component content + staleness indicator\n109\t        const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n110\t        const html = latestResult.rendered_html + `\n111\t            <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n112\t                <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n113\t                <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n114\t            </div>`;\n115\t\n116\t        card.innerHTML = html;\n117\t        return card;\n118\t    }\n119\t\n120\t    const card = document.createElement('div');\n121\t    card.className = 'topic-card';\n122\t    card.dataset.topicId = topic.id;\n123\t    card.dataset.cardId = cardId;  // Store card_id for in-place updates\n124\t\n125\t    const stalenessLevel = getStalenessLevel(staleness.seconds);\n126\t    card.classList.add(stalenessLevel);\n127\t\n128\t    const typeClass = topic.type || 'adhoc';\n129\t\n130\t    // Expose the topic type as a data attribute alongside data-topic-id so cards\n131\t    // are queryable by type with a robust, stable selector (e.g.\n132\t    // [data-topic-type=\"research\"]) rather than relying on class names. The DOM\n133\t    // verification suites assert both dataset attributes are present per card.\n134\t    card.dataset.topicType = typeClass;\n135\t\n136\t    let html = `\n137\t        <div class=\"topic-header\">\n138\t            <div class=\"topic-label\">${escapeHtml(topic.label)}</div>\n139\t            <div>\n140\t                <span class=\"topic-type ${typeClass}\">${escapeHtml(topic.type || 'adhoc')}</span>\n141\t                ${stalenessLevel !== 'fresh' ? `<span class=\"stale-badge ${stalenessLevel}\">STALE</span>` : ''}\n142\t            </div>\n143\t        </div>\n144\t    `;\n145\t\n146\t    // Add latest result if available\n147\t    if (latestResult) {\n148\t        const urgencyClass = latestResult.urgency || 'normal';\n149\t        html += `\n150\t            <div class=\"result-content\">\n151\t                <div class=\"result-summary\">\n152\t                    ${escapeHtml(latestResult.summary)}\n153\t                    <span class=\"urgency-badge ${urgencyClass}\">${escapeHtml(urgencyClass)}</span>\n154\t                </div>\n155\t        `;\n156\t\n157\t        if (latestResult.data) {\n158\t            html += `<div class=\"result-data\">${escapeHtml(JSON.stringify(latestResult.data, null, 2))}</div>`;\n159\t        }\n160\t\n161\t        html += `</div>`;\n162\t    }\n163\t\n164\t    // Add staleness indicator\n165\t    const timeAgo = formatStaleness(staleness.seconds);\n166\t    html += `\n167\t        <div class=\"staleness-indicator ${stalenessLevel}\">\n168\t            <span class=\"staleness-dot ${stalenessLevel}\"></span>\n169\t            <span>Updated ${timeAgo}</span>\n170\t        </div>\n171\t    `;\n172\t\n173\t    card.innerHTML = html;\n174\t    return card;\n175\t}\n176\t\n177\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n178\t// Shared escaping + small DOM helpers (text-node based — escaping contract)\n179\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n180\t\n181\tfunction escapeHtml(text) {\n182\t    const div = document.createElement('div');\n183\t    div.textContent = text == null ? '' : String(text);\n184\t    return div.innerHTML;\n185\t}\n186\t\n187\t/**\n188\t * Build a DOM element with tag + className + a list of children/strings.\n189\t *\n190\t * Strings are appended as TEXT NODES (never spliced into innerHTML), which is\n191\t * the escaping contract's literal form — a markup-looking log line renders as\n192\t * literal text instead of breaking layout. Use this for any value that must be\n193\t * inserted verbatim as text.\n194\t */\n195\tfunction el(tag, className, children) {\n196\t    const node = document.createElement(tag);\n197\t    if (className) node.className = className;\n198\t    (children || []).forEach(function (c) {\n199\t        if (c == null) return;\n200\t        if (typeof c === 'string' || typeof c === 'number') {\n201\t            node.appendChild(document.createTextNode(String(c)));\n202\t        } else {\n203\t            node.appendChild(c);\n204\t        }\n205\t    });\n206\t    return node;\n207\t}\n208\t\n209\t// Seconds → \"0s\" / \"12s\" / \"1m 05s\" elapsed label for pending cards.\n210\tfunction formatElapsed(ms) {\n211\t    const s = Math.max(0, Math.floor((ms || 0) / 1000));\n212\t    if (s < 60) return s + 's';\n213\t    const m = Math.floor(s / 60);\n214\t    const rem = s % 60;\n215\t    return m + 'm ' + (rem < 10 ? '0' : '') + rem + 's';\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n219\t// (2) First-run welcome card — built-in, zero DB dependence\n220\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n221\t\n222\t// Default example utterances used when the registry yields no project with a\n223\t// supported intent to derive one from. These never touch the component DB.\n224\tvar DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES = [\n225\t    'Has the options pipeline caught up?',\n226\t    'What is the state of the ibkr mcp?',\n227\t    'Queue up a research task on recent pipeline errors — no rush.'\n228\t];\n229\t\n230\t// Map a supported intent type to an example utterance fragment, so the welcome\n231\t// card's examples are drawn from the projects the router actually knows about\n232\t// (plan: Cold start & demo seed — \"2-3 example utterances drawn from those\n233\t// projects' supported intents\").\n234\tvar INTENT_EXAMPLES = {\n235\t    status: 'What is the status of {proj}?',\n236\t    action: 'Restart the {proj} deployment.',\n237\t    brainstorm: 'Brainstorm trade-offs for {proj}.',\n238\t    lookup: 'Pull up recent logs for {proj}.',\n239\t    'task-profile': 'Queue up a research task on {proj} — no rush.',\n240\t    'self-modification': 'Add an alias to the {proj} registry entry.',\n241\t    'monitoring-config': 'Watch {proj} for pod restarts.',\n242\t    reminder: 'Remind me to check {proj} in an hour.'\n243\t};\n244\t\n245\tfunction _deriveExamples(projects) {\n246\t    const out = [];\n247\t    const seen = {};\n248\t    // Prefer projects that carry a description (registry.yaml entries) so the\n249\t    // welcome examples reference real, described projects — not bare discovered\n250\t    // repos with auto-README blurbs.\n251\t    const ordered = (projects || []).slice().sort(function (a, b) {\n252\t        const ad = a && a.description ? 0 : 1;\n253\t        const bd = b && b.description ? 0 : 1;\n254\t        return ad - bd;\n255\t    });\n256\t    ordered.forEach(function (p) {\n257\t        if (out.length >= 3) return;\n258\t        (p.intent_support || []).forEach(function (intent) {\n259\t            if (out.length >= 3 || seen[intent]) return;\n260\t            const tmpl = INTENT_EXAMPLES[intent];\n261\t            if (!tmpl) return;\n262\t            seen[intent] = true;\n263\t            out.push(tmpl.replace('{proj}', p.slug || p.name || 'the project'));\n264\t        });\n265\t    });\n266\t    return out.length ? out : DEFAULT_EXAMPLE_UTTERANCES.slice(0, 3);\n267\t}\n268\t\n269\t// Cap the welcome-card project list so a 38-repo registry doesn't overwhelm the\n270\t// first frame. The first N are shown; an \"+N more\" note counts the rest.\n271\tvar WELCOME_PROJECT_CAP = 12;\n272\t\n273\t/**\n274\t * Build the first-run welcome card.\n275\t *\n276\t * @param {Array} projects  registry entries [{slug,name,description,intent_support,aliases}]\n277\t * @param {string} [description]  one-line description of aide-de-camp\n278\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .welcome-card element (data-builtin=\"welcome\")\n279\t *\n280\t * Renders even against an empty component library / DB — the project list comes\n281\t * from config/registry.yaml (served at /api/v1/registry), not the DB. The first\n282\t * real result replaces it (the canvas drops it once any topic card exists).\n283\t */\n284\tfunction createWelcomeCard(projects, description) {\n285\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card welcome-card');\n286\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'welcome';\n287\t\n288\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n289\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', ['👋']),\n290\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', ['Welcome to ADC'])\n291\t    ]));\n292\t\n293\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'builtin-desc', [\n294\t        description || 'A single input surface that routes what you say to the right project, fetches in parallel, and renders results as live cards.'\n295\t    ]));\n296\t\n297\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Registered projects']));\n298\t    const list = el('ul', 'builtin-project-list');\n299\t    (projects || []).forEach(function (p) {\n300\t        const slug = p.slug || p.name || 'project';\n301\t        const desc = p.description || '';\n302\t        const intents = (p.intent_support || []).join(' · ');\n303\t        const li = el('li', 'builtin-project');\n304\t        li.appendChild(el('strong', 'builtin-project-slug', [slug]));\n305\t        if (desc) li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(' — ' + desc));\n306\t        if (intents) li.appendChild(el('span', 'builtin-intents', [' ' + intents]));\n307\t        list.appendChild(li);\n308\t    });\n309\t    if (!(projects && projects.length)) {\n310\t        list.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-project', ['No projects registered yet.']));\n311\t    }\n312\t    card.appendChild(list);\n313\t\n314\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-section-title', ['Try asking']));\n315\t    const examples = el('ul', 'builtin-examples');\n316\t    _deriveExamples(projects).forEach(function (ex) {\n317\t        examples.appendChild(el('li', 'builtin-example', [ex]));\n318\t    });\n319\t    card.appendChild(examples);\n320\t\n321\t    return card;\n322\t}\n323\t\n324\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n325\t// Container decision — the headlessly-testable core of loadTopics()\n326\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n327\t\n328\t/**\n329\t * Decide what the canvas topic-container shows for a given card set.\n330\t *\n331\t * This is the shared render path index.html's ``loadTopics()`` calls after\n332\t * ``GET /api/v1/sessions/{id}/topics``: on a fresh session with zero cards it\n333\t * shows the first-run welcome card (built from the registry project list — no\n334\t * DB/result dependence); the moment any real card exists it shows only topic\n335\t * cards, which drops any welcome card (never alongside real cards). The caller\n336\t * clears the container and appends the returned nodes.\n337\t *\n338\t * Kept here (not inline in index.html) so the zero→welcome / first-result→drop\n339\t * contract is verifiable headlessly: ``tests/e2e/canvas_dom_runner.js`` drives\n340\t * this exact function (``--container`` mode) and asserts the welcome card\n341\t * renders from an empty card set and is absent once a real card is present.\n342\t *\n343\t * @param {Array} [cards]      card dicts from /topics (empty → welcome card)\n344\t * @param {Array} [projects]   registry entries for the welcome card\n345\t * @param {string} [description] one-line description for the welcome card\n346\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]}    child nodes for the container\n347\t */\n348\tfunction buildContainerChildren(cards, projects, description) {\n349\t    if (!cards || cards.length === 0) {\n350\t        return [createWelcomeCard(projects, description)];\n351\t    }\n352\t    return cards.map(function (c) { return createTopicCard(c); });\n353\t}\n354\t\n355\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n356\t// (3) Pending/ack cards — submit-time placeholder + per-thread pending\n357\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n358\t\n359\t/**\n360\t * Build a submit-time pending placeholder.\n361\t *\n362\t * Created LOCALLY at dispatch() submit time, BEFORE any server response, so a\n363\t * hung/wedged server still leaves a card on canvas to age (plan, Escalate\n364\t * Strand → Pending/ack card render path). It splits into per-thread pending\n365\t * cards when the dispatch ack (the /dispatch HTTP response) carries intent_ids.\n366\t *\n367\t * @param {string} utterance   the raw text being dispatched\n368\t * @param {number} createdAt   Date.now() at placeholder creation (ms epoch)\n369\t * @param {string} [pendingId] stable id for this placeholder (utterance id)\n370\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.placeholder (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n371\t */\n372\tfunction createPendingPlaceholderCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId) {\n373\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, 'Sending…');\n374\t    card.classList.add('placeholder');\n375\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'placeholder';\n376\t    return card;\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\t/**\n380\t * Build a per-thread pending card (one per dispatch-ack intent_id). Carries the\n381\t * per-source progress state ('3/5 sources in') and the elapsed-time footer.\n382\t *\n383\t * @param {string} intentId    the intent this thread tracks (result_created key)\n384\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance fragment/text\n385\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n386\t * @param {object} [progress]  {completed, total} initial per-source progress\n387\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .pending-card.thread (data-builtin=\"pending\")\n388\t */\n389\tfunction createPendingThreadCard(intentId, utterance, createdAt, progress) {\n390\t    const card = _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, intentId, 'Working on it…');\n391\t    card.classList.add('thread');\n392\t    card.dataset.pendingKind = 'thread';\n393\t    if (progress) _setProgress(card, progress);\n394\t    return card;\n395\t}\n396\t\n397\t/**\n398\t * Split a submit-time placeholder into per-thread pending cards on the dispatch\n399\t * ack. The /dispatch ack response carries ``intent_ids`` — one per routed\n400\t * thread — so this produces one per-thread pending card per intent_id, each\n401\t * inheriting the placeholder's ``createdAt`` (so the 30s aged timer is\n402\t * continuous across the split — the timer started at submit, not at ack) and\n403\t * the originating utterance. (plan, Escalate Strand → Pending/ack card render\n404\t * path: \"splits into per-thread pending cards when the dispatch ack arrives\".)\n405\t *\n406\t * This is the headlessly-testable core of the split: index.html's dispatch()\n407\t * calls it with the ack's intent_ids and replaces the placeholder DOM node with\n408\t * the returned cards; the Node DOM shim exercises it directly (no browser) to\n409\t * prove the split is one-card-per-thread.\n410\t *\n411\t * @param {string} utterance   the originating utterance text\n412\t * @param {number} createdAt   ms epoch inherited from the placeholder\n413\t * @param {string[]} intentIds the ack's intent_ids (one per routed thread)\n414\t * @returns {HTMLElement[]} one .pending-card.thread per intent_id\n415\t */\n416\tfunction splitPlaceholderToThreads(utterance, createdAt, intentIds) {\n417\t    return (intentIds || []).map(function (id) {\n418\t        return createPendingThreadCard(id, utterance, createdAt);\n419\t    });\n420\t}\n421\t\n422\t// Shared skeleton for both placeholder + per-thread pending cards.\n423\tfunction _createPendingCard(utterance, createdAt, pendingId, titleText) {\n424\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card pending-card');\n425\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'pending';\n426\t    card.dataset.createdAt = String(createdAt || 0);\n427\t    if (pendingId) card.dataset.pendingId = String(pendingId);\n428\t\n429\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n430\t        el('span', 'pending-spinner'),\n431\t        el('span', 'builtin-title pending-title', [titleText || 'Pending…'])\n432\t    ]));\n433\t\n434\t    if (utterance) {\n435\t        card.appendChild(el('p', 'pending-utterance', [utterance]));\n436\t    }\n437\t\n438\t    const progress = el('div', 'pending-progress');\n439\t    progress.style.display = 'none';\n440\t    card.appendChild(progress);\n441\t\n442\t    const elapsed = el('div', 'pending-elapsed', [\n443\t        formatElapsed(0) + ' elapsed'\n444\t    ]);\n445\t    card.appendChild(elapsed);\n446\t\n447\t    // Aged note (hidden until the 30s threshold) — see applyAgedTreatment().\n448\t    const agedNote = el('div', 'pending-aged-note');\n449\t    agedNote.style.display = 'none';\n450\t    agedNote.appendChild(document.createTextNode('Taking longer than expected — the server may be hung. '));\n451\t    const retry = el('button', 'pending-retry', ['Retry']);\n452\t    agedNote.appendChild(retry);\n453\t    card.appendChild(agedNote);\n454\t\n455\t    return card;\n456\t}\n457\t\n458\tfunction _setProgress(card, progress) {\n459\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-progress');\n460\t    if (!node || !progress) return;\n461\t    const completed = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.completed, 10) || 0);\n462\t    const total = Math.max(0, parseInt(progress.total, 10) || 0);\n463\t    node.textContent = completed + '/' + total + ' sources in';\n464\t    node.dataset.completed = String(completed);\n465\t    node.dataset.total = String(total);\n466\t    node.style.display = total > 0 ? '' : 'none';\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t/**\n470\t * Recompute the elapsed-time footer for a pending card against `now` (ms).\n471\t * Returns the elapsed ms. Pure: no timers, no globals beyond the card's own\n472\t * data-createdAt — so the headless harness can drive it via a mock clock.\n473\t */\n474\tfunction tickPendingElapsed(card, now) {\n475\t    const createdAt = parseInt(card.dataset.createdAt, 10) || 0;\n476\t    const elapsedMs = Math.max(0, (now || 0) - createdAt);\n477\t    const node = card.querySelector('.pending-elapsed');\n478\t    if (node) node.textContent = formatElapsed(elapsedMs) + ' elapsed';\n479\t    return elapsedMs;\n480\t}\n481\t\n482\t/**\n483\t * Apply (or remove) the aged-pending treatment based on elapsed ms vs the 30s\n484\t * threshold. Pure client-side — survives a hung server (no SSE dependency).\n485\t * Returns true if the card is currently flagged aged.\n486\t */\n487\tfunction applyAgedTreatment(card, now) {\n488\t    const elapsedMs = tickPendingElapsed(card, now);\n489\t    const aged = elapsedMs >= PENDING_AGE_THRESHOLD_MS;\n490\t    card.classList.toggle('aged', aged);\n491\t    const note = card.querySelector('.pending-aged-note');\n492\t    if (note) note.style.display = aged ? '' : 'none';\n493\t    return aged;\n494\t}\n495\t\n496\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n497\t// (4) Error / clarification family — Degraded-State UX, filled client-side\n498\t//     from SSE error events\n499\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n500\t\n501\t// Display metadata per error variant. title + icon + a one-line description\n502\t// template. Variants accept both snake_case and kebab-case keys (normalized in\n503\t// _errorVariant()). Maps directly to the Degraded-State UX matrix rows.\n504\tvar ERROR_VARIANTS = {\n505\t    router_unavailable: {\n506\t        title: 'Router unavailable',\n507\t        icon: '🔌',\n508\t        detail: 'The LLM proxy (ZAI) was unreachable at the router stage, so this utterance could not be routed.'\n509\t    },\n510\t    all_sources_failed: {\n511\t        title: 'No data available',\n512\t        icon: '📭',\n513\t        detail: 'Every fetch source failed or timed out, so there is nothing to synthesize.'\n514\t    },\n515\t    synthesis_failed: {\n516\t        title: 'Summary unavailable',\n517\t        icon: '⚠️',\n518\t        detail: 'The raw data was fetched, but the summary could not be produced.'\n519\t    },\n520\t    malformed_router_output: {\n521\t        title: \"Couldn't parse that into intents\",\n522\t        icon: '🧩',\n523\t        detail: 'The router returned output the system could not interpret.'\n524\t    },\n525\t    no_match: {\n526\t        title: 'No matching project',\n527\t        icon: '❓',\n528\t        detail: 'No registered project matched this request.'\n529\t    }\n530\t};\n531\t\n532\tfunction _normalizeVariant(errorType) {\n533\t    if (!errorType) return 'no_match';\n534\t    const key = String(errorType).trim().replace(/-/g, '_').toLowerCase();\n535\t    return ERROR_VARIANTS[key] ? key : 'no_match';\n536\t}\n537\t\n538\t/**\n539\t * Build an error/clarification card from an SSE error event payload.\n540\t *\n541\t * @param {ErrorCardData} data - Error card data structure\n542\t * @returns {HTMLElement} a .error-card element (data-builtin=\"error\")\n543\t *\n544\t * Per-source failure lists, the echoed utterance, raw degraded data, and any\n545\t * detail text are all inserted as text nodes (escaping contract).\n546\t */\n547\tfunction createErrorCard(data) {\n548\t    data = data || {};\n549\t    const variant = _normalizeVariant(data.error_type);\n550\t    const meta = ERROR_VARIANTS[variant];\n551\t\n552\t    const card = el('div', 'builtin-card error-card error-' + variant);\n553\t    card.dataset.builtin = 'error';\n554\t    card.dataset.errorType = variant;\n555\t    if (data.intent_id) card.dataset.intentId = String(data.intent_id);\n556\t    if (data.pending_id) card.dataset.pendingId = String(data.pending_id);\n557\t\n558\t    card.appendChild(el('div', 'builtin-header', [\n559\t        el('span', 'builtin-icon', [meta.icon]),\n560\t        el('span', 'builtin-title', [meta.title])\n561\t    ]));\n562\t\n563\t    const detail = data.detail || meta.detail;\n564\t    card.appendChild(el('p', 'error-detail', [detail]));\n565\t\n566\t    // Echo the utterance so nothing is ever lost (router_unavailable /\n567\t    // malformed_router_output rows). Rendered as text, never raw HTML.\n568\t    if (data.utterance) {\n569\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-utterance-wrap', [\n570\t            el('span', 'error-utterance-label', ['You said: ']),\n571\t            el('span', 'error-utterance', ['“' + data.utterance + '”'])\n572\t        ]));\n573\t    }\n574\t\n575\t    // Per-source failure list (all_sources_failed row).\n576\t    const sources = data.sources;\n577\t    if (Array.isArray(sources) && sources.length) {\n578\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-source-list');\n579\t        sources.forEach(function (s) {\n580\t            const name = (s && (s.name || s.source)) || 'source';\n581\t            const reason = (s && (s.reason || s.error)) || 'failed';\n582\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-source', [\n583\t                el('span', 'error-source-name', [name + ': ']),\n584\t                el('span', 'error-source-reason', [reason])\n585\t            ]));\n586\t        });\n587\t        card.appendChild(ul);\n588\t    }\n589\t\n590\t    // Degraded raw data (synthesis_failed row): fetched data is never discarded.\n591\t    if (data.data != null) {\n592\t        const raw = typeof data.data === 'string' ? data.data : JSON.stringify(data.data, null, 2);\n593\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-raw-label', ['Fetched data']));\n594\t        card.appendChild(el('pre', 'error-raw', [raw]));\n595\t    }\n596\t\n597\t    // No-match clarification: list the registered projects so the user can pick.\n598\t    if (variant === 'no_match' && Array.isArray(data.registered_projects) && data.registered_projects.length) {\n599\t        const ul = el('ul', 'error-project-list');\n600\t        data.registered_projects.forEach(function (p) {\n601\t            const slug = typeof p === 'string' ? p : (p.slug || p.name || 'project');\n602\t            ul.appendChild(el('li', 'error-project', [slug]));\n603\t        });\n604\t        card.appendChild(el('div', 'error-projects-wrap', [\n605\t            el('span', 'error-projects-label', ['Registered projects: ']),\n606\t            ul\n607\t        ]));\n608\t    }\n609\t\n610\t    // Recovery action button. Variant-aware label.\n611\t    const retryLabel = (variant === 'malformed_router_output' || variant === 'no_match')\n612\t        ? 'Edit & resend'\n613\t        : 'Retry';\n614\t    card.appendChild(el('button', 'error-retry', [retryLabel]));\n615\t\n616\t    return card;\n617\t}\n618\t\n619\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n620\t// Canvas Card Type Definitions\n621\t// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\n622\t\n623\t/**\n624\t * @typedef {Object} StuckCardData\n625\t * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that is stuck\n626\t * @property {string} stuck_reason - The reason why the bead is stuck (refusal reason)\n627\t * @property {number} [refusal_count] - Number of refusals that caused the stuck state\n628\t * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the stuck state\n629\t * @property {string} [action_hint] - Optional hint for user action to resolve\n630\t */\n631\t\n632\t/**\n633\t * @typedef {Object} FailedCardData\n634\t * @property {string} bead_id - The bead reference ID that failed\n635\t * @property {string} failure_reason - The reason why the task failed\n636\t * @property {string} [error_type] - Optional error type/category\n637\t * @property {string} [message] - Optional message describing the failure\n638\t */\n639\t\n640\t/**\n641\t * @typedef {Object} ErrorCardData\n642\t * @property {string} error_type - The error variant type\n643\t * @property {string} [utterance] - The original user utterance\n644\t * @property {string} [detail] - Error detail message\n645\t * @property {Array<{name?: string, source?: string, reason?: string, error?: string}>} [sources] - Failed sources list\n646\t * @property {*} [data] - Raw degraded data\n647\t * @property {string} [intent_id] - Associated intent ID\n648\t * @property {string} [pending_id] - Associated pending ID\n649\t * @property {Array<string|{slug?: string, name?: string}>} [registered_projects] - Projects for no-match variant\n650\t */\n651\t\n652\t/**\n653\t * @typedef {Object} PendingCardData\n654\t * @property {string} utterance - The utterance being processed\n655\t * @property {number} createdAt - Creation timestamp (ms epoch)\n656\t * @property {string} [pendingId] - Stable ID for this placeholder\n657\t * @property {number} [completed] - Number of completed sources (for progress)\n658\t * @property {number} [total] - {\n905\t    const card = document.querySelector('[data-approval-id=\"' + approvalId + '\"]');\n906\t    if (!card) return;\n907\t\n908\t    // Disable buttons\n909\t    const approveBtn = 'POST',\n918\t                headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n919\t                body: JSON.stringify({\n920\t                    approval_id: approvalId,\n921\t } catch (e) { return [['value', data]]; }\n1021\t    }\n1022\t    if (Array.isArray(obj)) {\n1023\t        return obj.slice(0, 50).map(function (v, i) { return [String(i), _stringify(v)]; });\n1024\t    }\n1025\t    if (typeof obj === 'object') {\n1026\t        return Object.keys(obj).slice(0, 50).map(function (k) { return [k, _stringify(obj[k])]; });\n1027\t    }\n1028\t    return [['value', String(obj)]];\n1029\t}\n1030\t\n1031\tfunction _stringify(v) {\n1032\t    if (v == null) return '';\n1033\t    if (typeof v === 'string') return v;\n1034\t    if (typeof v === 'number' || typeof v === 'boolean') return String(v);\n1035\t    try { return JSON.stringify(v); } catch (e) { return String(v); }\n1036\t}\n1037\t\n1038\t// Export shim: browser globals are already in place (these are top-level\n1039\t// function declarations).
